seoTitle: May Downs vs McDesme — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between May Downs and McDesme. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: May Downs vs McDesme: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in May Downs and McDesme.
heading: May Downs vs McDesme — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between May Downs, Queensland and McDesme, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: May Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to McDesme (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is May Downs or McDesme safer from flooding?
faq.answer: May Downs has a flood risk score of 23/100, while McDesme scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: May Downs scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. McDesme scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in May Downs or McDesme?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. May Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
